q: What port does the Cortex daemon listen on by default?
probes: lexical-semantic alignment on exact numeric fact

context_memories.text: The Cortex daemon binds to localhost on TCP 7437 by default. Override with CORTEX_PORT env var.

q: Which reranker model did we pick for Phase 2?

context_memories.text: Phase 2 selected cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 int8 (22MB, Apache 2.0) as the primary reranker.

q: How big is the BGE base embedding model we chose?
probes: "how big" → size in MB; noun-to-adjective paraphrase

context_memories.text: bge-base-en-v1.5 ships at roughly 438MB on disk; MTEB score ~64.23; replaces MiniLM-L12 as v0.6.0 default.

q: What's the L2-normalization output format for Qwen3 embeddings?

context_memories.text: Qwen3-Embedding output: L2-normalized 1024-dim fp32 vectors. Matryoshka-trained, truncation to any prefix-N dim works.

q: When did we discover the v0.5.0 benchmark inflation?

context_memories.text: On 2026-04-23 the CHANGELOG was corrected: raw adapter was 0.875, not 1.0. Misstatement found during v0.6.0 planning.

q: Why did we avoid Jina rerankers?

context_memories.text: Jina v2/v3 rerankers rejected — CC-BY-NC-4.0 license, incompatible with Cortex MIT.

q: How fast is power iteration on a 50K-edge graph for Cortex PPR?

context_memories.text: PPR power iteration benchmarks: 50K-edge graph with 10 seed nodes converges in ~35ms at α=0.5, tol=1e-4.

q: wht prt does cortex daemon lsiten on

context_memories.text: Cortex daemon defaults to 7437 on localhost.

q: whch reranker did we chose for phse 2

context_memories.text: Phase 2 chose ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2.

q: Wat SOTA numbr did Chonros hit

context_memories.text: Chronos High: 95.60% with Claude Opus 4.6 on LongMemEval-S.

q: wat GBNF grammer lib did we pick

context_memories.text: Picked llama-cpp-2 crate for GBNF grammar enforcement on Qwen2.5-1.5B in Rust.

q: hippoRAG PPR dampng factor

context_memories.text: HippoRAG 2 runs Personalized PageRank with α=0.5 over phrase∪passage graph.

q: wat cortex targt for DB size at 5k memorys

context_memories.text: v0.6.1 storage-hygiene target: 100 MB at 5K memories, down from 387 MB at 286.

q: wy did we ditch graphrag

context_memories.text: GraphRAG (Microsoft) rejected: cost prohibitive for local-first, wrong task shape for atomic memories.

q: Where is the CORTEX_HOME env var resolved?

context_memories.text: CortexPaths::resolve_with_overrides in daemon-rs/src/auth.rs reads CORTEX_HOME from env with CLI override precedence.

q: Which function implements the RRF fusion score?

context_memories.text: Phase 5 RRF fusion: rrf_score(rank) = 1.0 / (60.0 + rank), merged by rrf_fuse in daemon-rs/src/recall/rrf.rs.

q: What trait do all rerankers implement?

context_memories.text: daemon-rs/src/rerank/mod.rs defines trait Reranker { fn name(); fn rerank(query, candidates); fn model_size_mb(); fn warm(); }

q: Which migration adds the memory_links table?

context_memories.text: Phase 3.0 adds migration 013_memory_links_extraction.sql with memory_links table and extraction_audit audit table.

q: Where do we GC stale embedding rows?

context_memories.text: Phase 018 bloat-mitigation adds 'cortex admin embeddings-gc' CLI: drops rows where model NOT IN (active_models).

q: What Rust crate does Cortex use for GBNF-constrained decoding?
probes: crate name under crate ecosystem disambiguation

context_memories.text: llama-cpp-2 (utilityai/llama-cpp-rs, Apache-2.0/MIT) provides llama_cpp_2::json_schema_to_grammar() + LlamaSampler::grammar().

q: What PragmA flips the database to incremental reclamation?

context_memories.text: Migration 017: PRAGMA auto_vacuum = INCREMENTAL; VACUUM; then periodic PRAGMA incremental_vacuum(500) via daemon background job.

q: What was that SOTA ceiling we keep talking about for multi-hop?

context_memories.text: Real frontier is MemoryAgentBench CR-MH: SOTA ceiling ~6-7%. Every paradigm collapses from single-hop CR 60% to multi-hop ≤6%.

q: Remind me about the thing we're copying from Mastra?

context_memories.text: Phase 5.5 copies Mastra's Observer/Reflector pattern — stack-independent, directly reproducible in Rust + SQLite.

q: What did we say about the latest reranker findings in that paper?

context_memories.text: ArXiv 2409.07691 (NVIDIA, 2024) found MiniLM-L-12 reranker can REGRESS strong first-stage retrievers. Must test retriever-regression before shipping.

q: Who's doing the memory work that we're not?

context_memories.text: Chronos (PwC, arXiv 2603.16862) took the SOTA crown from Mastra with a structured event-retrieval layer. Relies on events calendar; -34.5pp on ablation.

q: What lever did we decide had biggest impact?

context_memories.text: Biggest single lever per Chronos ablation: ingest-time events extraction, -34.5pp when removed. Cortex Phase 3.0 addresses this.

q: Bring back what I said about the triangle thing
probes: imperative + noun phrase with vague noun

context_memories.text: Triangle judge mandatory: GPT-4o + Claude + local Qwen3-30B. Answerer must differ from judge family to avoid 5-25% self-preference bias.

q: That thing about licenses that ruled out that graph library

context_memories.text: GLiREL ruled out for Cortex Stage 1 RE: CC BY-NC-SA license incompatible with MIT. Replaced by GLiNER-multitask-v1.0 (Apache 2.0).

q: What's the total storage budget for Phase 3.0 models?
probes: target fact amid many size mentions

context_memories.text: bge-base 438MB; ms-marco-MiniLM 22MB; GLiNER ~400MB; Qwen2.5-1.5B 0.99GB; Qwen3-1.7B 1.11GB.
Phase 3.0 models total ~1.4 GB: 0.99 GB Qwen2.5-1.5B + 400 MB GLiNER. Shared with Phase 3.5 for zero extra storage.
SmolLM2-1.7B fallback: 1.02 GB if quality shortfall.
NuExtract-2.0-2B: 986 MB alternative fill model.
Phi-4-mini-instruct: MIT, ~1.4 GB, stronger MMLU but no weight-sharing.
Matryoshka truncation on Qwen3-embedding 32/64/128/256/512/1024 dims.

q: What's v0.6.0 target cut date?

context_memories.text: Current date 2026-04-23.
Week-6 checkpoint 2026-06-05.
v0.6.0 target cut Thursday 2026-07-16; 1-week buffer baked in.
v0.5.0 release was 2026-04-10 per git log.
Planning session 2026-04-23 locked scope.
Merge freeze begins 2026-03-05 for mobile release cut (prior project).

q: Which license did we rule OUT?

context_memories.text: GLiNER-multitask-v1.0 Apache 2.0 — primary pick.
gline-rs Rust crate Apache 2.0 — inference.
Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct Apache 2.0.
llama-cpp-2 Apache 2.0 / MIT.
GLiREL CC-BY-NC-SA — RULED OUT for commercial incompatibility.
bge-base MIT — embedding default.
ms-marco-MiniLM Apache 2.0 — rerank primary.

q: Which Rust graph library will Cortex use for PPR specifically?

context_memories.text: petgraph MIT/Apache: good for SCC, shortest-path, cycle detection.
nebari-graph: pedagogical, uniform teleport only.
petrel-graph: uniform teleport, not personalized.
igraph C binding: external dep, avoid.
For PPR specifically: ship custom ~250 LOC power-iteration Rust code in cortex-graph crate. petgraph::algo::page_rank lacks personalization argument.

q: What's the DEFAULT Cortex embedding model in v0.6.0?

context_memories.text: MiniLM-L6-v2: legacy, ~56 MTEB.
MiniLM-L12-v2: v0.5.0 default, ~59 MTEB.
Qwen3-embedding-0.6B: 1.2GB opt-in, ~68 MTEB.
bge-base-en-v1.5: v0.6.0 DEFAULT, 438MB, ~64 MTEB, MIT license.
nomic-embed-text: considered, not chosen.

q: Which adapter is the CANONICAL pure baseline?

context_memories.text: cortex-http: tuned, helper-augmented.
cortex-http-base: partial helpers, deprecated, 0.875 baseline.
cortex-http-pure: zero helpers, canonical baseline, single /recall call.
Any new quality claim must measure via cortex-http-pure with 5 purity gates green.

q: Which model did we pick for query decomposition in Phase 6?

context_memories.text: Qwen3-1.7B Q4_K_M: 1.11 GB, thinking/non-thinking toggle, Apache 2.0.
Qwen2.5-1.5B: reused for Phase 3.0/3.5/5.5 but no thinking mode.
SmolLM3-3B fallback: 1.8 GB, IFEval 76.7.
Phase 6 primary pick: Qwen3-1.7B for IRCoT query decomposition + answer synthesis.

q: What license is the crate we use to run GLiNER inference in Rust under?
probes: entity chain: GLiNER → inference library → license

context_memories.text: Phase 3.0 Stage 1 uses GLiNER-multitask-v1.0 for NER + RE.
GLiNER inference in Rust is done via gline-rs crate (github.com/fbilhaut/gline-rs).
gline-rs is Apache 2.0, built on ort + tokenizers + ndarray + regex.

q: What's the bundle size of the model we reuse for Phase 3.0 AND Phase 3.5?

context_memories.text: Phase 3.0 Stage 2 uses Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct GGUF via llama-cpp-2.
Phase 3.5 contextual prefixing reuses the same Qwen model for zero additional storage.
Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct Q4_K_M GGUF: 0.99 GB (bartowski repo).

q: What's the sqlite-vec version needed for the INT8 storage the bloat plan promises?

context_memories.text: Bloat research commits 7× DB shrink with embedding INT8 quantization.
INT8 vector storage requires sqlite-vec to support vec_int8 column type.
sqlite-vec v0.1.9 adds int8 + binary column types; Cortex currently on v0.1.6 must upgrade in Phase 1.5.

q: What algorithm drives the graph retrieval step that lifts CR-MH to 18%?

context_memories.text: Phase 5 targets MemoryAgentBench multi-hop CR 6% → 18-26% (3× SOTA).
Phase 5 builds HippoRAG-style associative retrieval over memory_links graph.
HippoRAG 2 uses Personalized PageRank with α=0.5; Cortex ships power-iteration deterministic PPR in cortex-graph crate.

q: Who founded the team behind the memory architecture we're mostly copying in Phase 5.5?

context_memories.text: Phase 5.5 is a Mastra-style Observer/Reflector consolidation loop.
Mastra AI built Observational Memory; hit 94.87% with GPT-5-mini on LongMemEval-S.
Mastra founders: Abhi Aiyer, Shane Thomas (CPO), Sam Bhagwat (CEO). All three built Gatsby.js.

q: What Cortex command clears out the sort of page bloat we saw on the author's DB?

context_memories.text: Author's cortex.db at 387MB with only 286 memories — page bloat.
~327 MB is unaccounted freelist — VACUUM would reclaim.
Phase 017 adds 'cortex admin vacuum-now' CLI + auto_vacuum=incremental default. Background job runs PRAGMA incremental_vacuum(500) on schedule.

q: What pool capacity does the Stage 2 background queue use for extraction?

context_memories.text: Phase 3.0 Stage 2 runs on a tokio mpsc channel.
Capacity constant STAGE2_QUEUE_CAPACITY = 128 in pipeline.rs.
Backpressure on full queue records extraction_audit event, does not block store.

q: What was the pure LongMemEval baseline BEFORE Phase 1 embedding upgrade?

context_memories.text: Phase 1 upgrade brings pure LongMemEval-S from ~0.82-0.84 estimate to ~0.87-0.90 with bge-base.
Post-Phase-1 pure baseline: ~0.87-0.90.

q: What SQL engine did we pick before switching?

Phase 1.5 of v0.6.0 upgrades to sqlite-vec v0.1.9 for int8 + binary support.

q: What graph algorithm did we consider BEFORE settling on power iteration?

context_memories.text: Memory-graph research evaluated Forward Push, bidirectional PPR, Monte-Carlo PPR, and power iteration.
Power iteration chosen for Cortex-scale (50K-500K edges) because Forward Push payoff starts at >1M edges.

q: What embedding model was default in v0.5.0 (not v0.6.0)?

v0.6.0 promotes bge-base-en-v1.5 to default; MiniLM profiles remain opt-in.
